title: pedicularis resupinata subsp. lasiophylla
intro: Pedicularis resupinata subsp. lasiophylla is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family, native to the western mountainous regions of North America. It is also known as mountain lousewort, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pedicularis resupinata subsp. lasiophylla include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat colds, fever, and other respiratory issues.
